data.title: Power & Infrastructure Split Corp. initiates an equity program to raise funds without specifics on amount to be raised.
data.description: According to recent reports, the unemployment rate in the country has reached 5.2%, which is a slight increase from the previous month. It was also informed that the GDP growth for the last quarter was 2.6%, exceeding expectations and showing positive signs for the economy. Additionally, the stock market experienced a slight dip, with the main index falling by 1.5% to 10,500 points. These economic indicators reveal a complex situation with some sectors showing growth while others are experiencing challenges. Experts are closely monitoring the situation to assess the overall health of the economy and predict future trends.

data.title: Headlines about data should be approached with skepticism as they can manipulate context. Home sales up 0.1%, but with margin of error at 90% confidence interval suggesting growth could be as much as 14.3% or -14.2%. Story framing is important to consider.
data.description: The article discusses the importance of critically analyzing news headlines, specifically focusing on two contrasting headlines about US new home sales data released last week. One headline from Reuters stated "US new home sales fall marginally in October," while another from the Wall Street Journal said "U.S. New Home Sales Pick Up After Summer, Delayed Data Say." Both headlines are factually accurate based on the data but showcase how different tones can be created by altering the context. The article highlights the margin of error in the Census's new home sales figures, derived from surveys, leading to reported figures being extrapolated from a sample and coming with a margin of error at a 90% confidence interval. This means that while new home sales reportedly increased by 0.1% in October, actual new home sales may have grown by as much as 14.3% or decreased by as much as 14.2% from the previous month. The base number is also subject to revision. The key takeaway is to approach news headlines with skepticism and consider the margin of error in data interpretations.

data.title: Foster Wallace Personal Injury Lawyers pledges $36,000 to Kansas City nonprofits through "12 Months of Giving" initiative.
data.description: A law firm in Kansas City has pledged to donate a total of $36,000 to support local nonprofits through monthly contributions and recognition efforts. The firm, Foster Wallace Personal Injury Lawyers, based in KANSAS CITY, Mo., made this commitment after their 12 Days of Giving campaign received significant positive feedback. The initiative includes regular donations along with highlighting the work of various nonprofits throughout the community.

data.title: Global organizations are entering a new reality of AI-driven decision-making, where human actions shape algorithms. Importantly support human-led values for AI success.
data.description: In Austin, Texas, global organizations are increasingly relying on AI for decision-making, leading to a shift in focus. They are not only creating AI systems for humans, but are now also shaping humans to fit into a machine-driven world. Everyday behaviors, leadership choices, and societal standards are becoming essential training inputs and models for algorithms. In this AI-dominated landscape, it is crucial to emphasize the significance of human involvement. Those who successfully integrate values and economic worth into AI development are likely to emerge as the frontrunners in this evolving environment.

data.title: Study: Stablecoins make Fed act as global retail bank, affecting central banks under 'impossible quartet'.
data.description: A recent paper by Sei Labs explores the global impact of dollar-pegged stablecoins by showcasing how they effectively transmit U.S. monetary policy worldwide. This phenomenon creates a complex challenge for central banks, known as the 'impossible quartet,' as they struggle to balance exchange rates, interest rates, monetary independence, and financial stability. The paper delves into the intricate dynamics of stablecoins and highlights the far-reaching influence they have on the global economy. This research sheds light on the significant role stablecoins play in shaping monetary policies across borders.

data.title: Hong Kong's Finance Secretary highlights need for regulations in crypto sector at WEF workshop in Davos.
data.description: During a private workshop at Davos, Finance Secretary Paul Chan emphasized the need for regulatory oversight in the digital assets sector to complement innovation.

data.title: Investigation launched by KSF into Charles River Laboratories officers and directors amid concerns.
data.description: A recent press release from New York and New Orleans dated January 20, 2026, revealed that Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq., a former Attorney General of Louisiana and current partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC (KSF), has initiated an investigation into Charles River Laboratories International, Inc. This marks a significant development in the legal landscape, highlighting concerns about potential issues related to the company's operations. Charles River Laboratories International, Inc. is facing scrutiny as KSF delves into the matter with thoroughness and diligence. The investigation underscores the growing importance of corporate accountability and transparency in today's business environment. Stay tuned for further updates as more information unfolds in this investigation.

data.title: Investigation initiated on Hasbro by Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC for potential misconduct by officers and directors.
data.description: In New York and New Orleans on January 20, 2026, Charles C. Foti, Jr., Esq., the former Attorney General of Louisiana and a partner at Kahn Swick & Foti, LLC, disclosed an ongoing inquiry by the firm into Hasbro, Inc. (NasdaqGS: HAS). The investigation, launched on October 26, 2023, aims to scrutinize the company's operations.
